seo.title: Responsible AI Governance for Agents in Instructor Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix responsible ai governance for agents in Instructor agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: Responsible AI Governance for Agents in Instructor manifests as failures in structured output validation, where agents produce invalid Pydantic models despite schema enforcement. Common issues include exhausted retries from malformed responses, missing required fields in partial extractions, hallucinated enum values, and inconsistent nested object structures. These failures undermine agent reliability and require governance mechanisms to detect, validate, and remediate outputs before deployment.

howItManifests.symptom: Instructor's from_openai() wrapper retries 3x but still raises ValidationError after max_attempts exceeded
howItManifests.impact: Agent halts execution, blocking downstream workflows and requiring manual intervention

howItManifests.symptom: Pydantic model returns None for required fields despite clear input data present
howItManifests.impact: Incomplete agent responses lead to cascading errors in multi-step agent workflows

howItManifests.scenario: Model hallucinating enum values outside schema
howItManifests.symptom: Instructor extracts enum values like 'INVALID_CHOICE' when schema defines ['approved', 'rejected', 'pending']
howItManifests.impact: Agents make decisions based on invalid categories, violating business logic

howItManifests.scenario: Nested object extraction producing inconsistent depth
howItManifests.symptom: Nested Pydantic models sometimes return partial trees (e.g., user.address missing street)
howItManifests.impact: Data pipelines fail validation, causing governance compliance violations

detection.name: Retry Exhaustion Monitor
detection.description: Detects when Instructor's validation retries are exhausted by catching ValidationError with retry metadata
detection.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel

class User(BaseModel):
    name: str
    age: int

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

try:
    user = client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o-mini',
        response_model=User,
        messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Extract user: John 35'}]
    )
except instructor.exceptions.WrongNumberOfRetriesError as e:
    print(f'Retry exhaustion detected: {e.validation_error}')
    raise

detection.name: Enum Hallucination Detector
detection.description: Validates extracted enum values against schema to catch hallucinations

detection.name: Nested Completeness Check
detection.description: Verifies all required nested fields are populated in Instructor extractions
detection.code:
from typing import Optional
from pydantic import BaseModel

class Address(BaseModel):
    street: str
    city: str

class User(BaseModel):
    name: str
    address: Optional[Address] = None

    def is_complete(self) -> bool:
        return (self.address is not None and 
                all([self.address.street, self.address.city]))

# Post-Instructor validation
user = client.chat.completions.create(
    response_model=User,
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'User: John, 123 Main St, NYC'}]
)

if not user.is_complete():
    raise ValueError('Nested extraction incomplete')

fixes.name: Fallback Partial Mode
fixes.description: Enable Instructor's partial mode to extract available fields when full validation fails
fixes.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class User(BaseModel):
    name: str
    age: int = Field(default=None)

client = from_openai(
    OpenAI(),
    mode=instructor.Mode.PARTIAL  # Fallback to partial extraction
)

user = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    response_model=User,
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Just John'}],
    max_retries=1
)
print(f'Partial success: {user.dict(exclude_unset=True)}')

fixes.name: Strict Enum Coercion
fixes.description: Configure Instructor to coerce hallucinated enums to closest valid value
fixes.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from enum import Enum

class Status(str, Enum):
    APPROVED = 'approved'
    REJECTED = 'rejected'

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

class Document(BaseModel):
    status: Status = Field(safer_underscore_names=True)

# Instructor auto-coerces invalid enums
doc = client.chat.completions.create(
    response_model=Document,
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'status: APPROVE'}],
    temperature=0.1  # Reduce hallucination
)

fixes.name: Nested Validation Retry
fixes.description: Implement custom retry logic for nested objects with specific prompts

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Responsible AI Governance for Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automates Instructor governance by wrapping client.chat.completions.create() calls with vex.verify(), providing real-time monitoring of validation failures, retry exhaustion, and schema violations. It captures detailed traces of Pydantic ValidationErrors, hallucinated enum values, and incomplete nested extractions, surfacing patterns across agent deployments. Vex's remediation engine suggests optimal temperature settings, retry configurations, and fallback modes based on historical failure patterns.

The platform generates governance dashboards showing MTTR for Instructor failures and compliance rates across agent fleets. Automated alerts trigger when validation success rates drop below 95%, with one-click fixes deploying updated Instructor configurations. Vex maintains audit trails for all agent outputs, ensuring Responsible AI compliance for production deployments.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import verify
from instructor import from_openai
from openai import OpenAI
from pydantic import BaseModel

# Install: pip install instructor vex-sdk
class AgentResponse(BaseModel):
    action: str
    confidence: float
    details: dict

# Wrap Instructor client with Vex
@verify()
def governed_agent(prompt: str) -> AgentResponse:
    client = from_openai(OpenAI())
    
    return client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o-mini',
        response_model=AgentResponse,
        messages=[
            {'role': 'system', 'content': 'Respond with valid schema ONLY.'},
            {'role': 'user', 'content': prompt}
        ],
        max_retries=2
    )

# Vex auto-detects: retry exhaustion, missing fields, enum hallucinations
response = governed_agent('Classify this transaction')

# Vex captures traces, suggests fixes, enforces governance

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK and wrap your Instructor calls to eliminate governance failures in 5 minutes.
